sections.paragraph:
ὄρεξις, εως, ἡ a condition of strong desire, longing, desire (ὀρέγω; Pla. et al.; LXX; Ath. 21, 1 and R. 21 p. 74, 19 al.; Hippol., Ref. 5, 14, 10); in its only occurrence in our lit. it is used in an unfavorable sense (Democr., Fgm. 219; Epicurus p. 161, 26 Us.; Lucian, Tyr. 4 τὰς τῶν ἡδονῶν ὀρέξεις χαλιναγωγεῖν; Herodian 3, 13, 6; Herm. Wr. 12, 4; Sir 23:6; Philo.—Of sexual desire Jos., Ant. 7, 169; Ps.-Clem., Hom. 3, 68; Orig, C. Cels. 3, 56, 24) οἱ ἄρσενες … ἐξεκαύθησαν ἐν τῇ ὀρ. αὐτῶν εἰς ἀλλήλους males … were inflamed with (their) desire for each other Ro 1:27.—DELG s.v. ὀρέγω. M-M. TW. Spicq.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Democr Democr , phil., V/IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Epicurus Epicurus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 721
